title: brassica longirostra var. transtagana
intro: Brassica longirostra var. transtagana, also known as Traganth, is a plant species that belongs to the Brassicaceae family and is native to the Iberian Peninsula. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and antiseptic properties. The plant's health benefits include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce inflammation, and act as an anti-inflammatory agent. In herbal preparations, Traganth is often used as a powder or infusion, and can also be found in combination with other herbs in teas, tinctures, and syrups to aid in respiratory health.
